ç.note: ich-laut; espeak emits precomposed U+00E7, the adapter NFD-split hands on c + U+0327, and this locale's NFC compose step restores U+00E7 before lookup

ø.note: German front rounded mid, surfaces long øː (schön)

øː.note: cross-inventory lens target; this locale does not own the category, so it appears only on the lens side

ɑu.note: cross-inventory lens target; this locale does not own the category, so it appears only on the lens side

ɒ.note: English-loan LOT vowel (Job)

ɜ.note: espeak's surface for the German -er reduction (Wasser)

ɦ.note: cross-inventory lens target; this locale does not own the category, so it appears only on the lens side

ɯ.note: cross-inventory lens target; this locale does not own the category, so it appears only on the lens side

ɹ.note: cross-inventory AmE lens target for the rhotic rules; must stay distinct from the German r/ɾ or the lens is a no-op

ɾ.note: espeak's German intervocalic tap surface rhotic

ʋ.note: cross-inventory lens target; this locale does not own the category, so it appears only on the lens side

ː.note: German contrastive length; rides the preceding segment

̃.note: combining tilde stays attached to its vowel (nasal loan vowels ɑ̃ œ̃); no precomposed form exists, so the locale's NFC compose step never merges it

̧.note: cedilla stays attached to its consonant

tʃ.note: cross-inventory lens target; this locale does not own the category, so it appears only on the lens side

ʁ.note: native German uvular R; espeak-de emits taps instead, so this arrives only via lens phone strings rendered on the German voice

ʔ.note: native German glottal onset; espeak-de never emits it, so this arrives only via lens phone strings rendered on the German voice

h.note: English /h/ lens target for pten.dorsal_r_h; the adapter never emits h in neutral phones (silent h is dropped upstream), so this only surfaces on the lens side; azure-symbol-probe-v1 receipt: accepted, HTTP 200

x.note: Brazilian dorsal-r velar fricative; the adapter emits x only for the guttural r (letter-x graphemes surface as ʃ/z/s/ks), so neutral rr renders as a velar r rather than ʃ; azure-symbol-probe-v1 receipt: accepted, HTTP 200

ø.note: front rounded absent; rule table targets e

ŋ.note: coda nasal; pt-BR realizes via nasalization

œ.note: rule-table symbol never observed in the pt inventory

purpose: Transliteration from the pinned Misaki adapter symbol inventories (frozen in 20260717-bilingual-g2p-reachability-v1) to Azure SSML IPA. Fidelity classes: exact = same category, notation-level change at most; approximate = nearest available category, documented loss; structural = not a phone; drop = intentionally omitted from ph strings. Every class except exact must be treated as unvalidated until the per-symbol Azure probe records an accept/reject receipt.
